Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: TurboQuant-WASM – Google's vector quantization in the browser 88 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Scientists observe an immune signaling complex forming inside cells 65 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. iNaturalist 509 upvotes, 123 comments
  4. Plague Ships 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. Embarrassingly simple self-distillation improves code generation 436 upvotes, 132 comments
  6. A new gene therapy is giving people born deaf the chance to hear 29 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. Some Unusual Trees 209 upvotes, 61 comments
  8. Artemis II crew take “spectacular” image of Earth 991 upvotes, 342 comments
  9. The Indie Internet Index – submit your favorite sites 35 upvotes, 7 comments
  10. Show HN: A game where you build a GPU 288 upvotes, 102 comments
  11. Training mRNA Language Models Across 25 Species for $165 83 upvotes, 26 comments
  12. How many products does Microsoft have named 'Copilot'? I mapped every one 42 upvotes, 11 comments
  13. OpenClaw privilege escalation vulnerability 490 upvotes, 231 comments
  14. The Cathedral, the Bazaar, and the Winchester Mystery House 120 upvotes, 48 comments
  15. Apple approves driver that lets Nvidia eGPUs work with Arm Macs 204 upvotes, 98 comments
  16. Components of a Coding Agent 94 upvotes, 40 comments
  17. Sopwith – 1984 Game (2000) 46 upvotes, 19 comments
  18. The most-disliked people in the publishing industry 81 upvotes, 39 comments
  19. Why the most valuable things you know are things you cannot say 64 upvotes, 30 comments
  20. Author of "Careless People" banned from saying anything negative about Meta 545 upvotes, 368 comments
  21. Claude Code Found a Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years 293 upvotes, 191 comments
  22. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 986 upvotes, 759 comments
  23. Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens 69 upvotes, 45 comments
  24. When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems 82 upvotes, 56 comments
  25. Electrical Transformer Manufacturing Is Throttling the Electrified Future 33 upvotes, 19 comments
  26. The CMS is dead, long live the CMS 84 upvotes, 62 comments
  27. Csp-toolkit – Python library to parse, analyze, and find bypasses in CSP headers 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model 90 upvotes, 79 comments
  29. German men 18-45 need military permit for extended stays abroad 276 upvotes, 433 comments
  30. Mbodi AI (YC P25) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 4:30am, 5 April 2026. Source here.
